title: Implement Orca in direct-style Scala 3 on Ox
acceptance: Flow scripts compile and read top-to-bottom without effect generators.

title: Flow scripts use a context-function DSL with an ambient FlowContext
acceptance: AsyncLocalStorage FlowContext resolves default services and overrides.

title: LLM backends are pluggable behind an `LlmBackend[B <: Backend]` trait
acceptance: Branded backend session identifiers reject cross-backend usage at typecheck time.

title: Split the codebase into `tools / flow / backend / runner` modules
acceptance: TypeScript package directories and import boundaries encode dependency direction.

title: Reshape the flow DSL so `import orca.{*, given}` is the whole ceremony
acceptance: Common flow scripts compile from the public package root.

title: Drive Claude Code via stream-json instead of TTY handoff
acceptance: Claude Tier 1 fixtures map inbound messages to normalized events.

title: Drive Codex via `codex exec --json` stdio JSONL
acceptance: Codex Tier 1 fixtures map JSONL events to normalized events and synthesized results.

selfHostingParity.acceptance: Codex option-mapping tests cover model, approval policy, read-only mode, self-managed git prompts, retry metadata, and structured output schema flags.
Codex session tests prove fresh thread capture, branded TypeScript session handles, and explicit unsupported resume failures for mismatched backends.
Codex structured-output tests pass JSON Schema, parse valid JSON, and return typed validation errors with raw output on invalid data.
Codex parser fixtures preserve stable tool-call ids, tool names, raw inputs, result content, and error status.
TypeScript mirrors Scala by observing backend CLI tool events and enforcing worktree/verification boundaries instead of replacing Bash/Edit/Read execution with an Orca-owned router.

title: Terminal output: event log + status bar
acceptance: Status bar and plain-mode terminal golden tests pass.

title: Announce typeclass for friendly summaries of structured outputs
acceptance: Structured result events emit raw output and optional summaries through a callback.

title: Prompts and helper-function convention
acceptance: Default prompts are exported and override-by-argument works.

title: Reviewer roster and aspects
acceptance: Reviewer prompts byte-match the Scala source.

title: MCP host bridge for mid-session user input
acceptance: Autonomous flows still reject human interaction, while explicit interactive Codex sessions use an Orca-owned ask_user MCP bridge.

selfHostingParity.acceptance: Interactive ask_user tests cover question emission, response routing, cancellation cleanup, and autonomous rejection.
Codex approval remains spawn-policy based until JSONL exposes approval request events; the matrix records the limitation.
The MCP ask_user bridge is backend-neutral at the API boundary but Codex-backed first in live execution; other live adapters remain explicit unsupported paths.

title: Persistent plans as the default for multi-task flows
acceptance: Plan path, recovery, and implement-loop Tier 2 golden tests pass.

selfHostingParity.acceptance: Plan tests persist prompts, task state, and recovery metadata without assuming a domain-specific task source.
Workflow tests detect unexpected child commits unless self-managed git is explicitly enabled.

title: OpenCode server driver
acceptance: OpenCode Tier 1 fixtures and lifecycle tests pass.

title: Drive Gemini CLI via `gemini --output-format stream-json` stdio JSONL
acceptance: Gemini Tier 1 fixtures pass without settings mutation for ask-user wiring.
